current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is tough due to very warm water temperatures at 79.7°F. Recent stocking improves odds slightly, but anglers should expect cautious trout behavior.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is the best window because moderate sky cover and light winds provide stable conditions, allowing trout to feed more comfortably despite warm water.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 79.7°F with a flow of 97.7 cfs, gage height at 3.69 ft, and turbidity measured at 8.6 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 22%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with no significant trend, wind averages 4.5 mph with gusts up to 13.8 mph, and rain probability is low at 15%.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will seek cold-water refuges and oxygenated pockets, favoring runs, seams, and structure. Present clean drifts using smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns and squirmy worms. Exercise caution as warm water increases trout stress and may reduce feeding activity.
